sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management
sources.version: NICE guideline NG19, last updated 11 October 2019 with selected 2023 recommendation amendments shown on the page
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
sources.population: Children, young people, and adults with diabetes, with age-specific foot-assessment recommendations.
sources.clinical_setting: Diabetes foot-risk assessment, active foot-problem documentation, multidisciplinary pathways, and follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for clinician examination of both feet, neuropathy, ischaemia, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot context, risk classification already assigned, and foot-review documentation. It does not auto-classify risk or generate referral or treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.6 — frequency, bilateral foot examination, and clinician risk stratification
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.10–1.3.13 — specialist assessment, reassessment, and information
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting completed foot protection assessment, vascular or biomechanical review, clinician-selected reassessment, information already provided, and an entered follow-up plan.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Neuropathic pain in adults: pharmacological management in non-specialist settings
sources.population: Adults with neuropathic pain managed in non-specialist settings.
sources.clinical_setting: Non-specialist assessment, treatment review, and specialist referral.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for adult neuropathic-pain review and treatment monitoring; partial for a generic neuropathy workflow and not directly validated for UAE prescribing or referral pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7 — assessment, treatment planning, referral, and review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.
